data.title: Yomiuri: IHI Aims to Double Nuclear Business Sales by End of 2030s
data.description:
By Daisuke Narahashi and Rina Ukita / Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writers

Japanese heavy industrial company IHI Corp. aims to double the sales of its nuclear business to about 100 billion yen by the end of the 2030s, IHI President and CEO Hiroshi Ide said during a recent interview with The Yomiuri Shimbun.

The company is set to incorporate the target in its next medium-term business plan that begins from the fiscal year ending March 2027. Amid a global trend of utilizing nuclear power, it aims to take advantage of the demand for the energy.

IHI manufactures pressure vessels and containment vessels for nuclear reactors. Nuclear power plants are now gaining attention for their ability to stably generate electricity without emitting greenhouse gases. In Japan, efforts to restart plants are accelerating, while overseas, an increasing number of new construction projects are underway. In the United States, major tech companies have begun utilizing nuclear power for procuring electricity for artificial intelligence services.

Ide stated that the nuclear power business has high growth potential in the future. "We will transfer personnel (to the nuclear business) and invest aggressively," the president said.

He revealed the company's plan of expanding the nuclear business by taking part in domestic projects to restart nuclear power plants, as well as new construction projects overseas, including one in Romania.

Regarding its aircraft engine business, which is one of the core sectors of IHI, Ide revealed his intention to put more focus on maintenance and repair works. Currently, the company carries out maintenance on about 70 engines a year. IHI aims to increase the figure to around 400 by the early 2030s.

Maintenance and repair services have an advantage of generating stable revenue after the products are sold. IHI manufactures aircraft engines and other products for Europe-based Airbus SE.

----

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Neither Dow Jones Newswires, MarketWatch, Barron's nor The Wall Street Journal were involved in the creation of this content.

YDN-m0000118793-1

data.title: Taiwan's Wistron to raise up to $923 million in share sale
data.description:
Taiwanese electronics manufacturer Wistron Corp is looking to raise up to $923 million by selling global depository shares that will be listed in Luxembourg, according to a term sheet reviewed by Reuters on Thursday.

The company, which is a supplier to Nvidia , is selling up to 250 million depository shares in a price range of $36.20 to $36.93, the term sheet said.

That is a discount of 4% to 6% to a closing stock price of NT$115 ($3.85) on Thursday.

Wistron did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Reuters.

The company plans to use the money raised in the share sale to buy raw materials in foreign currencies, the term sheet showed.

The shares are due to start trading on June 16.

Wistron said last month its new U.S. manufacturing facilities for customer Nvidia would be ready next year and that it was in talks with other potential customers. The facilities will produce high-performance computing and AI-related products.

($1 = 29.9060 Taiwan dollars)

data.title: Procter & Gamble to cut 7,000 jobs, WSJ reports
data.description:
Procter & Gamble said it would cut 7,000 jobs, or roughly 15% of its non-manufacturing workforce around the world, over the next two years, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday.

Reuters could not immediately verify the report.

data.title: Italian Construction Sector Growth Remains Marginal in May
data.description:
The HCOB Italy Construction PMI edged up to 50.5 in May 2025 from 50.1 in April, signaling a continued but modest expansion in the sector.

The uptick was driven by a marginal rise in new orders, supported by public tenders and momentum from the National Recovery and Resilience Plan.

Commercial construction remained the main growth driver, while residential building contracted for second month and civil engineering declined sharply, though less severe than last year.

Employment also rose at the fastest pace in 2025 so far, with firms hiring mostly on permanent contracts to meet demand.

However, purchasing activity edged down as companies relied on existing inventories amid rising input costs.

On prices, cost pressures intensified, driven by higher raw material, energy, and transportation costs, while delivery times worsened for the eighth straight month.

Despite headwinds, sentiment hit a one-year high as firms grew cautiously optimistic on future activity and expected project inflows.

data.title: Grasim Industries accepts bids for bond issue, bankers say
data.description:
India's Grasim Industries has accepted bids worth 10 billion rupees ($116.7 million) for bonds maturing in five years, three merchant bankers said on Thursday.

The company will pay an annual coupon of 6.56% on the issue and had invited bids from bankers and investors earlier in the day, they said.

Grasim Industries did not reply to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: German Construction Downturn Slightly Deeper in May
data.description:
The HCOB Germany Construction PMI slipped to 44.4 in May 2025 from 45.1 in April, signaling a slightly faster contraction in the sector.

Housing and commercial activity posted sharper declines, though civil engineering stabilized, ending a 20-month downturn.

New orders fell at a marginally faster pace amid continued uncertainty and elevated prices.

Meanwhile, employment remained under pressure, with job shedding accelerating to the fastest rate since February, accompanied by a renewed drop in subcontractor usage.

Purchasing activity continued to decline, though at the slowest rate in nearly three years, while supplier delivery times improved for the fourth consecutive month.

On prices, input cost inflation rose for the third straight month, reaching a 27-month high, though it remained below long-term averages.

Encouragingly, business sentiment turned positive for the first time since early 2022, supported by expectations of increased infrastructure spending.

SOCAR, Mubadala Energy discuss cooperation on new international upstream projects
data.description:
Mubadala Energy, a division of Abu Dhabi's sovereign wealth fund, is interested in working with the State Oil Company of Azerbaijan Republic (SOCAR) on international exploration and production projects, SOCAR president Rovshan Najaf said, as per Interfax."Within the framework of BakuEnergyWeek, we held a productive meeting with Mansoor Mohamed Al Hamed, Managing Director & CEO of Mubadala Energy, to explore cooperation opportunities across Azerbaijan's evolving energy sector," Najaf posted on Linkedin.The talks focused on "potential joint initiatives internationally in upstream projects and broader energy sector," he said."The meeting also underscored the mutual interest in fostering a long-term partnership with SOCAR. We value our collaboration with leading global energy companies and remain committed to sustainable growth and innovation in the energy sector," Najaf said.Mubadala Energy, which is headquartered in Abu Dhabi, operates in the Middle East, North Africa, Russia and Southeast Asia, producing 430,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day.SOCAR and Mubadala Energy are now working together on a project to develop the Tamar gas field off the coast of Israel, in which they hold stakes of 10% and 11%, respectively.

mrchub.com

data.title: Colle AI Expands Solana Infrastructure to Scale Creator-Led NFT Innovation
data.description:
Platform reinforces Solana-based features to empower scalable, AI-assisted NFT design and deployment

London, United Kingdom--(Newsfile Corp. - June 5, 2025) - Colle AI (COLLE), the multichain AI platform for NFT creation, has expanded its Solana-focused infrastructure to support a growing wave of creator-led digital innovation. The upgrade aims to simplify large-scale NFT deployments while ensuring low-latency, high-efficiency performance tailored for Solana's architecture.

Effortless NFT creation across chains-powered by Colle AI's intelligent multichain tools.

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

https://images.newsfilecorp.com/files/8833/254580_d4be9b9cb49e1b38_001full.jpg

The platform's enhanced Solana tools now offer faster minting, dynamic metadata integration, and greater flexibility in managing creator-defined asset traits. These features reduce development complexity while unlocking precision control for artists and developers. Backed by Colle AI's intelligent automation, creators can launch collections with chain-native optimization and cross-chain compatibility.

With this improvement, Colle AI ensures that Solana creators benefit from multichain fluidity, efficient cost handling, and seamless onboarding for both new and established users. The update continues Colle's mission to drive NFT accessibility through AI-enhanced tools that evolve with the Web3 landscape.

Colle AI's commitment to optimizing for Solana reinforces its position as a leading force in scalable, creator-first NFT infrastructure, making innovation more fluid and accessible than ever.

About Colle AI

Colle AI leverages AI technology to simplify the NFT creation process, empowering artists and creators to easily transform their ideas into digital assets. The platform aims to make NFT creation more accessible, fostering innovation in the digital art space.

Media Contact

Dorothy Marley

KaJ Labs

media@kajlabs.com

data.title: PRESSR: The Chance to win travel vouchers for NBK-Mastercard Holders when making foreign-currency purchases
data.description:
• Al Ballam: NBK is keen on rewarding its customers around the year with exclusive and valuable campaigns
  • Winners can redeem the vouchers at over 500 airlines and 1.2 million hotels around the world

In line with its continuous efforts to enrich its customers’ banking experience with unique campaigns and exceptional rewards that correspond to their different lifestyles and needs, the National Bank of Kuwait and Mastercard are partnering to reward 115 top spending customers with travel vouchers simply for using their NBK-Mastercard when spending in foreign currency during the period from June 1 to June 30, 2025.

This offer allows winners the chance to win a travel voucher worth up to KD 150, provided by Mastercard, which can be redeemed at more than 500 different airlines and over 2.1 million hotels around the world.

To get the chance to win, there are minimum spending limits during the campaign period, according to the card used in making the purchases, and they are as follows:

1. Spend a minimum of KD 550 if they hold any of the following cards:

  • NBK-Aura World Mastercard Credit Card
  • NBK Miles World Mastercard Credit Card
  • World Mastercard Credit Card
  • UEFA Champions League World Mastercard Credit Card issued by NBK

2. Spend a minimum of KD 300 if they hold any of the following cards:

  • NBK Platinum Mastercard Credit Card

3. Spend a minimum of KD 200 if they hold any of the following cards:

  • NBK Titanium Mastercard Credit Card
  • NBK Laki Titanium Mastercard Credit Card

Voucher distribution:

  • Mastercard Platinum & Titanium: 65 vouchers, valued at KD 75 each.
  • Mastercard World: 50 vouchers, valued at KD 150 each.

Commenting on this campaign, Anwar Al Ballam AVP- Cards services, at Consumer Banking Group, at National Bank of Kuwait said: “We are keen at NBK on rewarding our customers by launching exclusive campaigns around the year that guarantee an exceptional and distinguished experience for them beyond banking services and correspond to their different lifestyles and needs.”

Al Ballam also added: “Through this campaign in partnership with Mastercard, we strive to celebrate our NBK-Mastercard Credit Card and NBK-Mastercard Prepaid Card holders and enable them to gain massive benefits while helping them turn purchases into memorable trips.”

It is noteworthy that NBK’s Credit Cards are the best way to make payments, as holders access many benefits with ease, especially while shopping, including NBK Rewards and NBK Miles Programs, as well as Purchase Protection and Extended Warranty Services.

data.title: European Stocks Little Changed, ECB Awaited
data.description:
Both the STOXX 50 and STOXX 600 edged 0.1% higher on Thursday, as investors refrain from making big bets ahead of the European Central Bank’s monetary policy decision later in the day.

The ECB is widely expected to cut interest rates once more before potentially pausing to assess the broader impact of US trade policies under President Trump on the European economy.

On the data front, German factory orders unexpectedly rose in April, defying forecasts of a decline.

In corporate news, shares of LVMH (+1.1%), ASML Holding (+1.2%), L'Oréal (+0.7%), EssilorLuxottica (+1.1%), and Bayer (+3.5%) were among the session’s top performers.

data.title: U.S. Futures Flat, European Stocks Edge Up
data.description:
U.S. futures for the S&P 500 were flat and futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average were flat. Changes in futures do not necessarily predict movements after the opening bell.

In Europe, the Stoxx Europe 600 gained 0.1% in morning trading. Wise rose 8.7% and Vestas Wind Systems climbed 4.3%. On the other hand, Hemnet Group dropped 6.7%, and Avolta sunk 5.3%. The FTSE 100 added 0.1%. Other stocks in Europe were up as France's CAC 40 increased 0.2% and Germany's DAX climbed 0.1%.

The Wall Street Journal Dollar Index was flat at 95.21.

In commodities, Brent crude gained 0.4% to $65.13 a barrel, and WTI crude climbed 0.3% to $63.04 a barrel. The European benchmark price for natural gas, the Dutch futures contract TTF, was up 1.5% to 36.2 euros a megawatt hour.

The German 10-year Bund yield held steady at 2.525%, and the yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury rose by 1 basis point to 4.373% from 4.359%. Bond prices and yields move in opposite directions.

Stocks in Asia were mixed as Japan's Nikkei 225 index fell 0.5%, whereas Hong Kong's Hang Seng climbed 0.9%. China's benchmark Shanghai Composite climbed 0.2%.

-This article was automatically generated by MarketWatch.

Write to Barcelona Editors at barcelonaeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Azelis Group Target Cut to EUR17 From EUR21 by Berenberg
Azelis Group Cut to Hold From Buy by Berenberg
South Korea's New Government Could Face Challenges in U.S. Tariff Talks — Market Talk
data.description: South Korea's new Democratic Party government could face challenges in tariff talks with the U.S. if the Trump administration seeks to weaken Korean-Chinese trade linkages, Fitch Ratings analysts write in a note. The Democratic Party has historically sought balance in the country's ties with the U.S. and China, they say. They also see a lot of uncertainty over U.S. trade policy. South Korea is subject to a 25% reciprocal tariff that Trump has proposed but temporarily paused for negotiations. The U.S. may impose new levies on semiconductors, a major South Korean export item, in addition to current duties of 25% on autos and 50% on steel, Fitch adds. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0735 GMT - South Korea's new Democratic Party government could face challenges in tariff talks with the U.S. if the Trump administration seeks to weaken Korean-Chinese trade linkages, Fitch Ratings analysts write in a note. The Democratic Party has historically sought balance in the country's ties with the U.S. and China, they say. They also see a lot of uncertainty over U.S. trade policy. South Korea is subject to a 25% reciprocal tariff that Trump has proposed but temporarily paused for negotiations. The U.S. may impose new levies on semiconductors, a major South Korean export item, in addition to current duties of 25% on autos and 50% on steel, Fitch adds. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0729 GMT - If the European Central Bank reduces its inflation forecasts significantly when it announces a policy decision, it will likely limit the euro's gains against a weaker dollar, XTB's Kathleen Brooks says in a note. The ECB is widely expected to cut interest rates by 25 basis points, taking the deposit rate to 2.0%. Recent weak eurozone inflation data mean inflation forecasts are also expected to be reduced. "A large downward revision to the ECB's inflation forecasts could limit the euro's upside against the dollar in the short term," Brooks says. The euro has gained ground against the dollar over the past month, despite European bond yields lagging the U.S., she says. The euro rises 0.1% to $1.1423. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

0715 GMT - The U.S. dollar edges slightly higher against a basket of currencies in early European trade but remains weak and close to six-week lows. The dollar fell on Wednesday after weak U.S. ADP private payrolls data, while trade-policy uncertainty also weighs on the U.S. currency. "Interestingly, the U.S. dollar is one of the few assets reflecting trade pessimism," says Swissquote Bank's Ipek Ozkardeskaya in a note. The DXY index rises 0.1% to 98.849, having hit a six-week low of 98.583 earlier this week. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0702 GMT - A deceleration in Philippines' headline inflation to 1.3% in May from 1.4% the previous month raises the risk of a policy rate cut this month, says Aris Dacanay of HSBC Global Research. The peso's relative strength could also give Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas room to lower policy rates, the economist says in a note. That said, the June 19 meeting remains a tough call as upside risks to inflation remain. Dacanay points to the lower house of Congress's approval of an across-the-board minimum-wage hike this week. Policymakers are also considering raising tariff rates on rice. If this materializes, BSP may need to reconsider the pace of its easing cycle, he adds. HSBC's baseline scenario is for the central bank to pause its easing cycle in June. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0658 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly lower. IG calls the OMXS30 down 0.1% at around 2508. Major stock exchanges were somewhat mixed on Wednesday, SEB analysts say in a note. Weak U.S. economic data increased expectations that the Federal Reserve will cut its policy rate in the coming months. Stock markets in Asia have moved in slightly different directions overnight and futures are close to zero in both the U.S. and Europe. ECB policymakers are expected to lower on Thursday and SEB doesn't expect the central bank to commit to a given rate path ahead. OMXS30 closed at 2510.47, OMXN40 at 2413.70 and OBX at 1499.55. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0616 GMT - France's 10 billion euro-12 billion euro issuance of long-dated government bonds on Thursday looks set to weigh on Bunds in the earlier part of the session, Commerzbank Research's Hauke Siemssen says in a note. However, this potential rise in Bund yields could be used for tactical long positions ahead of the European Central Bank's meeting, the rates strategist says. Money markets fully price a 25-basis-point rate cut by the ECB on Thursday, bringing the deposit rate to 2.00%. Significant falls in U.S. Treasury yields on Wednesday following weak data left German Bunds unfazed. The 10-year Bund yield is flat at 2.521% shortly after market open, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0610 GMT - U.S. Treasury yields are stabilizing after sharp declines Wednesday prompted by weak data. The two-, 10- and 30-year Treasury yields are trading flat at 3.879%, 4.367% and 4.890%, respectively, according to Tradeweb. Wednesday's declines of 8 basis points in the two-year yield and 9.5 basis points in the 10-year yield were prompted among others by a below-consensus ADP jobs report, which puts Friday's nonfarm payrolls data under even bigger scrutiny than usual. However, further signs of weakness in the labor-market data are needed before the Federal Reserve can cut interest rates, Metzler analysts Leon Ferdinand Bost and Uwe Hohmann say in a note. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0537 GMT - The European Central Bank is expected to give no clear signal for July, with risks leaning toward signaling a pause, says Pictet Wealth Management's Nadia Gharbi in a note. "Importantly, by the July meeting, several key developments will help clarify the policy debate," the senior economist says. These include the conclusion of the 90-day tariff pause, possibly more information on the German budgets for 2025 and 2026, two additional PMI reports scheduled for June 23 and July 24, and one more inflation report due July 1, she says. Pictet WM expects the ECB to lower its growth and inflation forecasts for 2025 and 2026 and conclude that monetary policy easing is warranted. Pictet WM expects a 25-basis-point rate cut today. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0532 GMT - Ongoing declines in eurozone inflation and consistently dovish language from European Central Bank members make a rate cut appear a done deal on Thursday, says Lazard Asset Management's Ronald Temple in a note. The ECB has previously described 1.75%-2.25% as the range that would be considered neutral monetary policy--one that neither stimulates nor cools the economy. "Any signals of a change in this view would be surprising," the chief market strategist says. "I continue to expect rates to be reduced to 1.5% by year end given a more aggressive U.S. trade posture against the European Union." His forecast is marginally below money market expectations for rates to end the year around 1.70%, according to LSEG data. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0513 GMT - The Reserve Bank of India is likely to cut its policy repo rate by 25 bps to 5.75% on Friday, Commerzbank Research analysts say in a research report. India's central bank downgraded its growth forecast to 6.5% at April's monetary-policy meeting, citing "exceptional uncertainties," the analysts note. Also, India's inflation is subdued, with headline inflation in April at 3.2% on-year, below RBI's 4% midpoint target. This gives the RBI room to cut rates to "cushion the downside risks to growth," the analysts add. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0511 GMT - China's travel economy is expected to exceed 1 trillion yuan by 2026, Citi analysts say in a research note. "The government appears to be looking at services exports via 'Travel in China' as a potential offset to tariffs on 'Made in China' goods exports," they write. "China travel" is now macro-relevant, generating revenue of $94 billion, or about 0.5% of GDP in 2024, according to the analysts. That is up 78% from 2023. Meanwhile, inbound tourism is unlikely to contribute to inflation significantly. Citi estimates that inbound travelers accounted for 0.5% of total retail sales last year, still too small to move the needle. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0507 GMT - The Malaysian ringgit may find support from sustained investment flows, U.S.-China tariff de-escalation, and a softer dollar, MUFG analyst Lloyd Chan says. Robust domestic investment, driven by high realization rates and strong local participation, could continue to offset global trade headwinds, he says in a note. Ringgit bonds also remain attractive to foreign investors, with rising foreign holdings and strong demand seen in recent auctions, he adds. MUFG forecasts USD/MYR at 4.20 in 2Q, 4.23 in 3Q, and 4.19 in 4Q. The pair is 0.1% lower at 4.2440. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Wall Street Journal - June 5
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Wall Street Journal. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday signed a sweeping travel ban on 12 countries, largely in the Middle East and Africa, and introduced more-limited travel restrictions on seven others, reintroducing a controversial immigration policy that came to define the early days of his first term.

- Citi will reduce its technology workforce in China by about 3,500 as part of ongoing restructuring to streamline global operations.

- UniCredit said the European Union wouldn't launch an in-depth investigation into its $10 billion-plus bid for Banco effectively clearing the deal to take over its smaller rival.

- U.S. President Donald Trump ordered an investigation into Joe Biden's actions as president, including his use of an autopen, alleging that his predecessor's aides hid evidence of what he called Biden's "serious cognitive decline."

- Kimberly-Clark is nearing a sale of its Kleenex and tissue businesses outside of North America for around $3.5 billion, part of an attempt by the maker of Scott toilet paper and Huggies diapers to focus on more profitable areas, according to people familiar with the matter.

- The Trump administration is redirecting a key antidrone technology earmarked for Ukraine to American forces, a move that reflects the Pentagon's waning commitment to Kyiv's defense.

data.title: Russian media outlet says investigators search its editorial office
data.description:
A Russian media outlet in the Urals city of Yekaterinburg said on Thursday that investigators were conducting searches in its editorial office.

Ura.ru, one of the largest media outlets in the Urals, said searches were also being conducted in the flats of several journalists.

"Investigators are interested in sources of information about events of a criminal nature in the Sverdlovsk region," Ura.ru said in a statement.

There was no official comment from investigators.

Ura.ru published video of officials in uniform in an office and masked investigators with sledge hammers.

data.title: China’s Lopal to supply LFP material to EVE Energy Malaysia from 2026–30
data.description:
China’s Jiangsu Lopal Tech said it will supply 152,000 metric tons of lithium iron phosphate (LFP) material to EVE Energy Malaysia, as per Chemweek.LFP material is a highly sought material as it is used to build electric vehicle (EV) batteries. China’s Lopal, a leading cathode material producer, will supply LFP from its Indonesian plant from 2026 until 2030.The project’s ability to secure significant funding despite the downturn in the LFP market underscored a strong market confidence in its potential, industry sources said to Platts, part of S&P Global Commodity Insights.This agreement will enable the company to build a long-term partnership with downstream players, Lopal said. Under the agreement, the buyer is allowed to adjust expected demand within a certain range, provided that the advance notice period is satisfied. The seller agreed to accept all orders within the fluctuating range of the expected demand, Lopal said.The first phase of Lopal’s Indonesian plant with capacity of 30,000 metric tons per year was put into operation in early 2025 and has achieved mass production, with shipments delivered to overseas customers' factories. It currently maintains a high-capacity utilization rate.Meanwhile, the second phase, with a capacity of 90,000 metric tons per year, has begun construction and is expected to complete building works and equipment commissioning by the end of this year.The Indonesian plant has signed another supply agreement with Blue Oval Battery Park, in the US state of Michigan, in January. The agreement did not mention any contracted volumes.In December 2024, the Indonesian plant and Lopal’s Changzhou plant amended an agreement to provide a total of 260,000 metric tons of LFP material to LG Energy Solution during 2024–28.

mrchub.com

data.title: HUTCHMED hits 2-month high on drug application for kidney cancer treatment
data.description:
** Shares of biopharmaceutical group HUTCHMED (China) Ltd jump 9.7% to HK$26, their highest since April 2

** Stock on course for third consecutive session of gains

** HUTCHMED and Іnnovent Biologics say in a joint statement that China National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) has accepted for review the New Drug Application for the combination of fruquintinib and sintilimab for treatment of renal cell carcinoma in kidney cancer

** Fruquintinib is co-developed and co-commercialized in China by HUTCHMED and Eli Lilly

** Sintilimab is co-developed and co-commercialized by Іnnovent and Eli Lilly

** Hong Kong's healthcare index (.HSCIH) falls 1.6%, Hang Seng Biotech Index drops 1.8%

** YTD, HUTCHMEd stock up 5.8%

data.title: Market Chatter: China Considers Major Airbus Jet Order
data.description:
China is in talks to buy between 200 and 500 Airbus aircraft amid a planned Beijing visit by European leaders in July, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday.

The potential deal, involving narrowbody and widebody jets, with the European aeronautics company would mark one of the largest in aviation history and could serve as a strategic signal to Washington amid rising US-China trade tensions, according to the report.

Meanwhile, US rival Boeing has been unable to secure a major order from China since at least 2017 due to trade tensions and other issues, Bloomberg wrote.

China's state-run aircraft procurement body usually negotiates aircraft purchases on behalf of the country's airlines, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.
